subject: Product Management
title: Pivoting Toward Fit
description: A short lesson to help you apply Pivoting Toward Fit.

hook.text: Instagram started as Burbn, a location-sharing app with too many features. Nobody cared about most of them.

buildup.text: Founders Kevin Systrom and Mike Krieger noticed one thing users loved: sharing photos with filters.

discovery.text: They stripped away everything else and relaunched as Instagram — just photos and filters. It hit PMF instantly.

twist.text: The pivot wasn't a new idea. It was recognizing which part of the old idea users already loved.

climax.text: Instagram reached 25,000 users on day one and one million in two months. Facebook acquired it for $1 billion.

punchline.text: Sometimes PMF is hiding inside a failed product.

quiz.question: How did Instagram find product-market fit?
options: By stripping away everything except the photo-sharing feature users loved
By adding more features to the Burbn app
